D-18 PILLAR PAGE (A12) WAS THE MOST DENSE BUT DID NOT SPAWN NEW DRIFT — limited-companies pillar synthesises 8 use-case forks + operational framework + 5 extraction routes + 10 cost-side cons + 4 alternatives + 30+ cross-link grid in a single page. The pillar architecture held without depth-duplicating any specialist page (per Stage 1b/2 anti-templating note). Forward-link grid covers BTL/HMO/SPV/extraction/group/incorporation-mechanics/lever-pillar/alternatives/decision/ECCTA specialists. Reinforces the three-pillar architecture (A1 incorporation mechanics + A4 7-lever lever-map + A12 entity pillar).

D-19 HONEST-FRAMING DISCIPLINE HELD ACROSS A10 + A11 + A12 — Three pages required honest pro-and-con framing rather than advocacy: A10 (hybrid LLP DOES NOT solve Section 24 for founder + own-LtdCo); A11 (HMO incorporation works for some configurations, loses for others — Example 5 honest counterexample); A12 (limited-companies pillar Examples 1+5 explicitly hold pro-incorporation and pro-personal outcomes side-by-side). The discipline mattered most for A10 where popular advisor commentary tends toward Section-24-workaround promotion; RUN authoring led with the mixed-membership trap as the dominant frame.

anti_templating_check: Six pages read as a set. Each has distinct framing per the Stage 1 differentiator anchors: A7 = taxonomy/definitional layer (PA 1890 four-tests; s.2(1) negative; SA800 trigger); A8 = eligibility/definitional pillar for CT group relief (three-section composite; Sch 18 overlay; consortium relief); A9 = policy-update overlay for the LLP cluster (BlueCrest + post-BlueCrest narrowing); A10 = honest-framing hybrid-LLP page (mixed-membership trap as dominant frame; three residual legitimate uses); A11 = HMO-specific decision helper (6 pros + 10 cons; post-MDR-abolition; HMO-specific capital allowance upside); A12 = LtdCo PILLAR/HUB (navigational synthesis across 8 use-case forks + 30+ forward-link grid). Boilerplate sections (voice + style, 19-step workflow scaffolding, six-check quality bar) are verbatim per §4.8 / §7; substantive content distinct per pick.
cross_pick_coordination_check: A7 (partnership taxonomy) is the parent definitional layer for partnerships and LLPs generally; A9 (salaried-member rules — individual-member side) + A10 (mixed-membership rules — corporate-member side) are sibling FA-2014-anti-avoidance regimes for LLPs (both reference A7 as parent and cross-reference each other for the dual-regime simultaneous-firing scenario in Examples); A8 (group-relief eligibility) is the multi-company structural layer for LtdCo groups; A11 (HMO incorporation pros-and-cons) is the HMO-specific decision page that funnels to A1 incorporation mechanics (M2-A-B1) and forward to existing HMO-incorporation mechanics page; A12 (limited-companies pillar) sits above all of A1-A11 + A19 + future A14/A15 + B5 as the cluster entry point. Cross-link directionality enforced: pillar → specialist; specialist → pillar; sibling → sibling within FA-2014 regime cluster. No cross-pick contradictions identified.
